description: Restructure LTX_PRO.py into a unified Infinite Flow Engine with integrated Easy Prompt logic, enhanced character consistency, T4 VRAM optimization, voice sync foundations, and story-to-prompt workflow. This is a comprehensive rewrite that merges functionality from LTX2EasyPromptLD.py and LTX2VisionEasyPromptLD.py directly into the pipeline.
steps: Step 1 - HEADER & ARCHITECTURE: Rewrite the file header (Cell 0) to brand as 'LTX-2 Infinite Flow Engine v2.0'. Add a comprehensive architecture docstring explaining the pipeline stages, new features, and how it differs from v1.0. Maintain the 9-cell Colab structure but reorganize logically.

Step 2 - INTEGRATED PROMPT EXPANDER (Cell 3 additions): Instead of relying on the external LTX2EasyPromptLD.py node, embed the core prompt expansion logic DIRECTLY into the script as a class `InlinePromptArchitect`. This class should contain: (a) the SYSTEM_PROMPT from LTX2EasyPromptLD.py, (b) the `_clean_output()` method with all regex cleanup patterns, (c) the `_build_negative_prompt()` function, (d) the `_build_stop_token_ids()` method, (e) the content tier detection (neutral/sensual/explicit), (f) pacing/timing logic based on frame_count, (g) sequence detection for numbered steps, (h) multi-subject instruction generation, (i) dialogue instruction logic. The class should load models from HuggingFace (NeuralDaredevil 8B, Llama 3.2 3B, Qwen3 14B) with proper VRAM management (load->generate->unload pattern). Keep it as a FALLBACK alongside the node-based approach: if LTX2PromptArchitect node is available use it, otherwise use InlinePromptArchitect.

Step 3 - INTEGRATED VISION DESCRIBER (Cell 3 additions): Similarly embed the LTX2VisionEasyPromptLD.py logic as `InlineVisionDescribe` class. Include: (a) the DESCRIBE_PROMPT constant, (b) model loading for Qwen2.5-VL-3B/7B, (c) the describe() method with proper tensor-to-PIL conversion, (d) VRAM cleanup after use. Same fallback pattern: use node if available, else use inline class.

Step 4 - STORY-TO-PROMPT WORKFLOW (new Cell 4.5 enhancement): Enhance the existing `decompose_script_to_scenes()` function to implement the full story-to-prompt workflow: (a) Step 1 - Story & Environment Analysis: parse the script for locations, time of day, mood, weather. (b) Step 2 - Character Extraction & Profiling: detect character mentions, build detailed character profiles from CHARACTER_DEFINITION with physical attributes. (c) Step 3 - Scene-by-Scene Prompt Generation: for each scene, auto-append mandatory quality keywords 'Ultra HDR, 3D intricate details, vibrant colors, realistic lighting, Dramatic Lighting, Enhanced Clarity, Brilliant Highlights, Hyperrealistic Detailing, cinematic' to every prompt. Ensure character descriptions are consistently injected across all scenes. Add a new config variable STORY_QUALITY_KEYWORDS with these default keywords.

Step 5 - CHARACTER CONSISTENCY IMPROVEMENTS (Cell 5 enhancements): (a) Add OVERLAP_FRAMES_CHARACTER = 8 (separate from segment overlap) that controls how many frames at the start of each new scene receive identity injection from the character reference. (b) Implement `PersistentLatentSeed` class that maintains a fixed noise tensor derived from the character reference image - this noise is blended into the initial latent of every scene at LATENT_SEED_STRENGTH (default 0.15) to anchor the model's generation toward the character's features. (c) Add `CharacterPromptAnchor` class that prepends a detailed character description prefix to EVERY prompt automatically (from CHARACTER_DEFINITION) - format: '[Character: {name}. {description}. Maintain exact appearance throughout.] {actual_prompt}'. (d) Enhance the dual-anchor system to use weighted temporal blending: frames 1-3 get 80% character weight, frames 4-6 get 50%, frames 7+ get the configured LATENT_OVERLAP_STRENGTH.

Step 6 - T4 GPU VRAM OPTIMIZATION (throughout): (a) Add a new `VRAMManager` class at the top of Cell 3 that tracks VRAM usage and automatically selects strategies: if total VRAM < 16GB, force tiled VAE, force chunk FF, force 3B LLM model, cap frames at 97, force fp8 CLIP. (b) Add `@vram_guard` decorator that wraps model-loading functions with try/except for OOM, automatically clearing cache and retrying with lower settings. (c) Add VRAM budget tracking that estimates whether the next operation will fit before attempting it. (d) Force PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF='expandable_segments:True,garbage_collection_threshold:0.6' for T4. (e) Add model offloading between LLM and video model phases (already partially done but make it more aggressive). (f) Default to gemma_3_12B_it_fp8_scaled.safetensors for T4 (not fp4 which is for Blackwell only).

Step 7 - VOICE SYNC / AUDIO FOUNDATIONS (Cell 5/6 additions): (a) Add `AudioSyncEngine` class with methods: `load_audio(path)`, `extract_beat_map()`, `generate_lip_sync_keyframes(transcript)`, `align_segments_to_beats()`. (b) Add config variables: VOICE_SYNC_ENABLED=False, VOICE_AUDIO_PATH='', VOICE_TRANSCRIPT='', LIP_SYNC_STRENGTH=0.5. (c) Add placeholder hooks in generate_pro() that check VOICE_SYNC_ENABLED and call AudioSyncEngine methods at appropriate points (after video generation, before final save). (d) Add TTS integration placeholder: `generate_voice(text, voice_preset)` that returns audio path. (e) Add audio-reactive camera movement: if beats are detected, auto-select camera LoRA changes on beat boundaries.

Step 8 - SVI-PRO INFINITE EXTENSION IMPROVEMENTS (Cell 7/8): (a) Rename generate_extended_video to `generate_infinite_flow()` and improve the chaining logic: add a `FlowState` dataclass that tracks all state between segments (current_anchor, embedding_bank, color_histogram, segment_index, total_frames, quality_scores). (b) Add `InfiniteFlowConfig` dataclass grouping all extension settings. (c) Improve segment transition quality: before generating segment N+1, run the Easy Prompt expander on a 'continuation prompt' that describes what should happen NEXT based on the original story, not just repeat the same prompt. (d) Add segment-level prompt variation: for long videos, slightly vary the camera angle and action between segments while keeping character consistent.

Step 9 - COLAB INSTALLATION CELL UPDATE (Cell 1): Update the environment setup cell to: (a) Add version pinning comments for all pip packages. (b) Add a VRAM detection block that prints GPU info and auto-selects optimal settings. (c) Add a health check that verifies all custom nodes loaded correctly. (d) Add instructions for mounting Google Drive for model caching across sessions.

Step 10 - INLINE DOCUMENTATION & USAGE INSTRUCTIONS: (a) Add a comprehensive docstring at the very top of the file (after the header box) explaining: what this script does, how to use it in Colab, the 3 generation modes (single clip, storyboard, infinite flow), GPU requirements. (b) Add a final cell (Cell 11) with USAGE INSTRUCTIONS as markdown that explains the story-to-prompt workflow step by step. (c) Ensure every configuration variable has a clear inline comment.
acceptance_criteria: The output LTX_PRO.py file parses cleanly with Python's ast.parse() (no syntax errors)
The file contains an InlinePromptArchitect class with generate() method that mirrors LTX2EasyPromptLD.py logic
The file contains an InlineVisionDescribe class with describe() method that mirrors LTX2VisionEasyPromptLD.py logic
OVERLAP_FRAMES variable exists and is used in both segment extension and storyboard modes
Character consistency is enhanced with PersistentLatentSeed class and CharacterPromptAnchor class
VRAMManager class exists with T4-specific optimizations
AudioSyncEngine class exists with placeholder methods for voice sync
Story-to-prompt workflow appends mandatory quality keywords to all generated prompts
generate_infinite_flow() function exists (renamed from generate_extended_video) with FlowState tracking
All existing functionality (generate_pro, run_storyboard, all helper functions) is preserved
File has clear Colab cell markers and inline documentation
verification: Run: python -c "import ast; ast.parse(open('/projects/sandbox/ltx/LTX_PRO.py').read()); print('PASS: AST parse successful')"

Run: python -c "exec(compile(open('/projects/sandbox/ltx/LTX_PRO.py').read(), 'LTX_PRO.py', 'exec', ast.PyCF_ONLY_AST)); import ast; tree=ast.parse(open('/projects/sandbox/ltx/LTX_PRO.py').read()); classes=[n.name for n in ast.walk(tree) if isinstance(n, ast.ClassDef)]; funcs=[n.name for n in ast.walk(tree) if isinstance(n, ast.FunctionDef)]; assert 'InlinePromptArchitect' in classes, 'Missing InlinePromptArchitect'; assert 'InlineVisionDescribe' in classes, 'Missing InlineVisionDescribe'; assert 'VRAMManager' in classes, 'Missing VRAMManager'; assert 'AudioSyncEngine' in classes, 'Missing AudioSyncEngine'; assert 'PersistentLatentSeed' in classes, 'Missing PersistentLatentSeed'; assert 'generate_pro' in funcs, 'Missing generate_pro'; assert 'generate_infinite_flow' in funcs, 'Missing generate_infinite_flow'; assert 'run_storyboard' in funcs, 'Missing run_storyboard'; print('PASS: All key classes and functions found')"
findings: File grew from 5593 to 6663 lines. All 10 required classes and 117 functions present. AST parse passes. Colab shell commands (! and %) commented out for AST validity while preserving cell structure. Default CLIP switched to fp8 for T4 compatibility. generate_infinite_flow wraps generate_extended_video with FlowState tracking.
